name: Shatavari
benefit: Women's reproductive tonic; supports lactation, menstrual balance, and menopausal symptoms. Classic 'queen of herbs' in Ayurveda.
recipe: 1 tsp powder in warm milk with a little ghee and honey, once or twice daily.
sourcing: Grows wild across India and Sri Lanka. Dried root powder widely available in Ayurvedic pharmacies.

name: Brahmi
benefit: Medhya rasayana — cognitive and memory tonic. Modern trials show improved recall and reduced anxiety.
recipe: 500 mg dried herb or 300 mg standardized extract (to 20% bacosides) twice daily with meals.
sourcing: Marshlands of India and Southeast Asia.

name: Guduchi (Giloy)
benefit: Immunomodulator, hepatoprotective, and antipyretic. Traditionally used in recurrent fevers and autoimmune modulation.
recipe: Fresh stem decoction: 10 g stem boiled in 200 ml water, reduced by half; taken morning empty stomach.
sourcing: Climbing shrub across India; fresh stems or standardized extract.

name: Neem
benefit: Blood purifier, antimicrobial, antiparasitic. Used topically for skin conditions and systemically for metabolic support.
recipe: Leaf paste topically on acne/eczema; or 500 mg capsule orally twice daily (short courses).
sourcing: Tropical tree across India; leaves, oil, and bark all used.

name: Amalaki (Amla)
benefit: Highest natural vitamin C food; rasayana (rejuvenator), eye and hair tonic, hypolipidemic.
recipe: 1 tsp powder in warm water morning; or fresh fruit daily in season.
sourcing: Deciduous tree of India; fresh fruit in winter, dried year-round.

name: Haritaki
benefit: 'King of medicines' in Ayurveda. Mild laxative, digestive tonic, rejuvenative.
recipe: ½ tsp powder with warm water at bedtime; not in pregnancy.
sourcing: Fruit of the chebulic myrobalan tree, Himalayan foothills.

name: Bibhitaki
benefit: Respiratory tonic and mild expectorant; the third component of Triphala.
recipe: Usually taken as part of Triphala rather than alone.
sourcing: Fruit of the bellirica tree, common across India.

name: Gotu Kola
benefit: Brain tonic, wound healing, connective-tissue support; modern trials for venous insufficiency.
recipe: 1 tsp dried herb as tea, twice daily; or topical cream on slow-healing wounds.
sourcing: Marshy areas of India, Sri Lanka, Indonesia; fresh or dried.

name: Licorice (Yashtimadhu)
benefit: Demulcent for throat and gut; mild adrenal support, harmonizes formulas.
recipe: Chew small stick; or 1–3 g root decoction. Avoid with hypertension.
sourcing: Root of the licorice plant; Mediterranean and Asian origin.

name: Kutki
benefit: Potent hepatoprotective and cholagogue; used in jaundice and chronic liver disease.
recipe: 250–500 mg root powder twice daily under supervision.
sourcing: Himalayan alpine herb (2500–4500 m); sustainably sourced root.

name: Bhumyamalaki
benefit: Liver tonic; traditionally for jaundice, hepatitis B adjunct, and kidney stones.
recipe: Fresh whole plant decoction, or 500 mg capsule twice daily.

name: Manjistha
benefit: Blood purifier and lymphatic mover; used in skin conditions, menstrual irregularities.
recipe: ½ tsp powder twice daily in warm water; or topical paste.
sourcing: Root of Indian madder; dried and powdered.

name: Vacha
benefit: Medhya rasayana for speech and cognition; stimulant, carminative. Use cautiously — some varieties contain β-asarone.
recipe: Very small dose (100–250 mg) under practitioner guidance; or topical oil on joints.

name: Jatamansi
benefit: Calming nervine, promotes sleep, reduces palpitations and anxiety.
recipe: 250–500 mg root powder at bedtime with warm milk.
sourcing: Endangered Himalayan rhizome; buy from sustainable suppliers only.

name: Punarnava
benefit: Diuretic, anti-inflammatory; used in edema, kidney support, anemia.
recipe: Fresh herb juice 10–20 ml; or 500 mg powder twice daily.

name: Gokshura
benefit: Urinary tract tonic; traditionally for male vitality, modern research mixed for testosterone.
recipe: 500 mg fruit powder twice daily, ideally in short courses.

name: Shilajit
benefit: Mineral-pitch rasayana; used for energy, male vitality, and as a mineral tonic.
recipe: 200–500 mg purified resin in warm water/milk once daily.
sourcing: Himalayan mineral exudate; must be purified (shuddha shilajit). Heavy-metal testing essential.

name: Kapikacchu
benefit: Natural L-DOPA source; traditional nervine tonic, studied for early Parkinson's and male fertility.
recipe: 1 tsp seed powder daily; standardized extract often used in formulas.

name: Bacopa (Brahmi)
benefit: Duplicate of Brahmi — see main entry.

name: Vidari Kanda
benefit: Rejuvenative tonic, galactagogue, supports female reproductive system.
recipe: 1–3 g tuber powder in warm milk daily.

name: Arjuna
benefit: Cardiac tonic; bark extract improves left ventricular function in clinical trials.
recipe: 500 mg standardized bark extract twice daily with food.
sourcing: Bark of the arjuna tree, Indian subcontinent.

name: Pippali
benefit: Warming digestive and respiratory stimulant; increases bioavailability of other herbs.
recipe: ¼–½ tsp powder with honey for coughs.
sourcing: Dried unripe fruit of long-pepper vine.

name: Trikatu
latin: Three pungents (Piper nigrum, Piper longum, Zingiber officinale)
benefit: Classical digestive formula; kindles agni (digestive fire), improves absorption.
recipe: ½ tsp powder with warm water before meals.
sourcing: Compound; make from 3 equal parts powdered.

name: Sitopaladi churna
benefit: Classic Ayurvedic cough formula: rock candy, bamboo silica, pippali, cardamom, cinnamon.
recipe: ½ tsp with honey 2–3× daily for dry cough.

name: Chyawanprash
benefit: Immunity-boosting herbal jam centered on amla with ~50 herbs; daily rasayana.
recipe: 1 tsp morning, followed by warm milk.
sourcing: Traditional recipe; brands vary in quality.

name: Mahanarayan oil
benefit: Medicated oil for joints, muscles, and nerves; used in abhyanga (self-massage).
recipe: Warm and massage into affected joints or whole body.

name: Bhringraj
benefit: Hair tonic, scalp health, liver support.
recipe: Topical oil massage into scalp; or 1 tsp powder daily for liver.
sourcing: Common wet-field herb across India and East Asia.

name: Tagar
benefit: Sedative and anxiolytic; Himalayan relative of European valerian.
recipe: 250–500 mg root powder 30 min before sleep.

name: Shankhpushpi
benefit: Medhya (cognitive); used for memory, concentration, and anxiety.
recipe: Syrup form 10 ml twice daily; or 1 g powder in milk.

name: Kumari (Aloe Vera)
benefit: Women's tonic, gastric demulcent, topical healer. See main aloe vera entry.

name: Karela (Bitter Melon)
benefit: Hypoglycemic; traditional diabetes remedy with modern clinical support.
recipe: 30 ml fresh juice morning empty stomach; or steamed vegetable.

name: Methi (Fenugreek)
benefit: Blood-sugar support, galactagogue, digestive tonic.
recipe: 1 tsp soaked seeds overnight, eaten morning; or sprouted.
sourcing: Seed widely available; also as leafy vegetable.

name: Jamun
benefit: Seed powder lowers blood sugar; fruit is cooling and astringent.
recipe: ½ tsp seed powder in water twice daily before meals.
sourcing: Purple Indian blackberry, seed dried and powdered.

name: Gymnema
benefit: 'Sugar destroyer' — blocks sweet taste and supports blood-sugar regulation.
recipe: 400 mg standardized extract twice daily with meals.

name: Vasaka
benefit: Bronchodilator and expectorant; classical remedy for asthma and cough.
recipe: 5–10 ml leaf juice with honey; or syrup 10 ml twice daily.
sourcing: Leaves and roots; plant common across India.

name: Kantakari
benefit: Cough, asthma, and respiratory congestion.
recipe: Decoction of whole plant, 30 ml twice daily.

name: Pushkarmool
benefit: Classical Ayurvedic cardiac and respiratory herb; supports chest pain and breathlessness.
recipe: 500 mg root powder twice daily.

name: Shallaki (Boswellia)
benefit: Anti-inflammatory resin; clinical support for osteoarthritis and IBD.
recipe: 300–500 mg standardized extract (AKBA) twice daily.

name: Nirgundi
benefit: Topical analgesic for joint pain; also used in respiratory conditions.
recipe: Warmed leaves applied to painful joints; or leaf oil massage.

name: Guggul
benefit: Cholesterol reduction, anti-inflammatory, joint support.
recipe: Purified guggul 500 mg twice daily; traditional vatis.
sourcing: Gum-resin of the mukul myrrh tree, western India.

name: Ananta
benefit: Blood purifier, cooling herb for skin and urinary conditions.
recipe: Root decoction 50 ml twice daily; or cold infusion.
sourcing: Twining shrub of India; root used.

name: Khadira
benefit: Astringent and antiseptic; dental, skin, and diarrhea support.
recipe: Bark decoction as mouthwash or gargle; internal as powder 500 mg.

name: Haridra (Turmeric)
benefit: See turmeric main entry.

name: Kutaj
benefit: Classical anti-diarrheal; bark effective against amoebiasis.
recipe: Bark decoction 50 ml 3× daily for acute dysentery (short course).
sourcing: Small tree of India; bark used.

name: Bilva
benefit: Unripe fruit for diarrhea/IBS; ripe for nutrition and constipation.
recipe: Unripe fruit powder 1 tsp with water twice daily; or ripe fruit pulp as food.
sourcing: Sacred bael tree; fruit harvested at specific ripeness.

name: Kusha grass
benefit: Cooling diuretic; classical urinary support.
recipe: Decoction of fresh grass as cooling drink.

name: Apamarga
benefit: Diuretic, emmenagogue, and anti-inflammatory; topical for wounds.
recipe: Whole-plant decoction 30 ml twice daily. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Moringa
benefit: Nutrient-dense superfood; supports blood sugar, energy, breast milk.
recipe: 1 tsp leaf powder daily in smoothies or warm water.
sourcing: Fast-growing tree; leaves, seeds, and pods all used.

name: Yastimadhu ghrita
benefit: Medicated ghee for gastritis, peptic ulcers, and eye health.
recipe: 1 tsp morning empty stomach.

name: Saraswatarishta
benefit: Fermented brain tonic for memory, focus, and anxiety.
recipe: 15 ml after meals twice daily, diluted with equal water.

name: Dashamoola
benefit: Balances vata; used in nervous-system disorders, pain, and postpartum.
recipe: 20 ml decoction morning and evening; or as kwath.
sourcing: Compound of 10 roots — traditional pharmacy blend.

name: Triphala guggul
benefit: Triphala + guggul for weight management and lipid support.
recipe: 1 tablet twice daily after meals.

name: Kanchanar guggul
benefit: Traditional formula for thyroid nodules, cysts, and glandular swellings.
recipe: 2 tablets twice daily for 1–3 months; practitioner supervision.

name: Avipattikar churna
benefit: Hyperacidity, heartburn, and hemorrhoids; mild laxative.
recipe: ½–1 tsp with warm water at bedtime.

name: Hingvashtak churna
benefit: Digestive stimulant with asafoetida; relieves bloating and gas.
recipe: ¼ tsp mixed with ghee in first bite of food.

name: Sarivadi vati
benefit: ENT support — sinusitis, rhinitis, earache.
recipe: 1 tablet twice daily.

name: Karpooradi taila
benefit: Topical analgesic oil with camphor; sprains and muscle pain.
recipe: Massage into affected areas.

name: Mahasudarshan churna
benefit: Antipyretic herbal blend for recurrent fevers.
recipe: 1 g powder with honey 2–3× daily during acute fever.

name: Kumari asava
benefit: Fermented aloe tonic; women's health, digestion, mild laxative.
recipe: 15 ml after meals twice daily with equal water.

name: Drakshasava
benefit: Fermented grape tonic; cardiac and digestive support, convalescence.
recipe: 15 ml after meals twice daily.

name: Ashwagandharishta
benefit: Fermented ashwagandha tonic for nervous-system support.
recipe: 15 ml after meals twice daily with water.

name: Brahmi ghrita
benefit: Cognitive support; used in nasya (nasal) therapy and orally.
recipe: ½ tsp morning on empty stomach.

name: Anu taila
benefit: Medicated oil for nasal application; sinuses, headaches, hair/eye support.
recipe: 2 drops each nostril daily after warming.

name: Bhumi amla
benefit: Duplicate of Bhumyamalaki.

name: Rudraksha
benefit: Traditionally worn for cardiac and calming effects; magneto-therapeutic claims preliminary.
sourcing: Seed of the rudraksha tree, Nepal/India.

name: Shatavari Kalpa
benefit: Milk-based tonic for postpartum and lactation.
recipe: 1 tsp in warm milk daily.

name: Tulasi ark
benefit: Distilled tulsi for cough, immunity, and mild fevers.
recipe: 5–10 drops in warm water 2–3× daily.

name: Pushkara guggul
benefit: Cardiac formula combining pushkarmool and guggul.
recipe: 500 mg twice daily.

name: Lodhra
benefit: Astringent for women's health — leucorrhea, menorrhagia, acne.
recipe: ½ tsp bark powder with rice-water twice daily.
sourcing: Bark of Lodhra tree, Himalayan foothills.

name: Ashoka
benefit: Classical women's herb; menstrual regulation and pain.
recipe: Ashokarishta 15 ml twice daily during menstrual complaints.
sourcing: Bark of the ashoka tree; CITES-listed in some regions.

name: Chandraprabha vati
benefit: Urinary tract tonic; prostate, cystitis, diabetic nephropathy adjunct.
recipe: 2 tablets twice daily with water.

name: Sudarshan ghan vati
benefit: Antipyretic formula; recurrent fevers and chronic low-grade fever.
recipe: 2 tablets 2–3× daily during acute phase.

name: Bhallataka
benefit: Strong rejuvenative and anti-tumor traditionally; caustic — practitioner use only.
attribution: Toxic if unprocessed — professional preparation required

name: Musta
benefit: Digestive stimulant, diuretic; classical for fever and dysmenorrhea.
recipe: Root decoction 30 ml twice daily.
sourcing: Tuber of nut-grass, common weed worldwide.

name: Chitrak
benefit: Warming digestive stimulant, supports metabolism.
recipe: Small doses (250 mg) in formulas; not alone in excess.
sourcing: Root of Plumbago; used in classical formulas.

name: Kapikacchu churna
benefit: Duplicate of Kapikacchu.

name: Varuna
benefit: Urinary tract tonic; traditional for kidney stones and benign prostatic conditions.
recipe: Bark decoction 30 ml twice daily.
sourcing: Bark of the three-leaved caper tree.

name: Ajwain
benefit: Carminative, antispasmodic; ajwain water for infant colic and bloating.
recipe: ½ tsp seeds steeped in hot water as tea after meals.
